title: Dynamic Programming States, Transitions, and Reconstruction · Trace Retrieval
summary: Optimize resource allocation where overlapping choices defeat local heuristics. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a bottom-up optimizer with reconstructed decisions after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define minimal subproblem state that preserves all future-relevant information.
Derive transitions from exhaustive final choices and valid base cases.
Choose memoization or tabulation from reachability, order, depth, and memory needs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Dp Subproblems correctness-and-cost proof for this review task before implementing it.

stimulus.title: Dp Correctness case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Choose from items (value,weight) [(6,2),(10,3),(12,4)] with capacity 7. Define state meaning, transition order, and reconstruction before optimizing memory.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Testing many inputs replaces a DP correctness argument.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prove state meaning and transition completeness by induction. Artifact under review: a bottom-up optimizer with reconstructed decisions.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Dp Correctness, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
